channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Madhuku says police deployed 50 officers to block NCA meeting over CAB3 campaign @nehandaradio
item.description: Professor Lovemore Madhuku says police deployed more than 50 officers to prevent an NCA leadership meeting focused on opposing the recently enacted Constitution Amendment Act No. 6 of 2026, but claims the gathering went ahead at a secret venue. The post Madhuku says police deployed 50 officers to block NCA meeting over CAB3 campaign appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Zimbabwe Sees Record Growth in Mobile and Internet Access Despite Affordability Challenges
item.description: Zimbabwe Records Major Digital Growth, but High Internet and Smartphone Costs Remain a Challenge Zimbabwe has reached its highest levels of mobile phone ownership and internet connectivity, according to a new national survey. However, the high cost of smartphones and internet services continues to limit digital access for many people, particularly in rural areas. The [...] The post Zimbabwe Sees Record Growth in Mobile and Internet Access Despite Affordability Challenges appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Escalating Crackdown on Zimbabwe’s Opposition Amidst Constitutional Upheaval: CIOs Thoroughly Beat up MPs
item.description: HARARE — A chilling pattern of intimidation and violence is unfolding across Zimbabwe, casting a dark shadow over the nation’s political landscape. In a series of coordinated events that highlight the growing intolerance for dissent, opposition figures and civil society leaders are finding themselves in the crosshairs of suspected state security agents. The recent assault [...] The post Escalating Crackdown on Zimbabwe’s Opposition Amidst Constitutional Upheaval: CIOs Thoroughly Beat up MPs first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Thomas Mapfumo’s nephew defends Mukanya over US$1 million Chivayo deal @nehandaradio @wicknellchivayo
item.description: The national debate over Thomas Mapfumo’s decision to accept Wicknell Chivayo’s reported US$1 million performance package has intensified, with his nephew rallying behind the music icon while prominent lawyer Brighton Mutebuka argues the move has damaged his moral standing. The post Thomas Mapfumo’s nephew defends Mukanya over US$1 million Chivayo deal appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Zulu Monarch King Misuzulu Praises Mnangagwa’s Leadership Despite Worsening Citizens’ Suffering
item.description: Tinashe Sambiri| Zulu monarch King Misuzulu kaZwelithini has praised President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s leadership following talks in Zimbabwe, even as the... Continue reading "Zulu Monarch King Misuzulu Praises Mnangagwa’s Leadership Despite Worsening Citizens’ Suffering" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Prime Homes Pvt Ltd Directors In Court For for US$2 Million Fraud
item.description: By A Correspondent| Prime Homes Pvt Ltd directors have appeared at the Harare Magistrates court on allegations of selling company... Continue reading "Prime Homes Pvt Ltd Directors In Court For for US$2 Million Fraud" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe expects 1.4 million citizens to return from South Africa as repatriation gathers pace @nehandaradio
item.description: Zimbabwe says almost 100,000 citizens have returned from South Africa since May, but officials expect up to 1.4 million could eventually come home as xenophobic tensions continue. The post Zimbabwe expects 1.4 million citizens to return from South Africa as repatriation gathers pace appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Zimbabwe unveils ICT Access Report at household, individual level
item.description: Obey Musiwa and Sekai Manyere Zimbabwe has launched the 2025 ICT Access by Households and Use by Individuals Survey Report, providing fresh insights into how households and individuals are accessing... (via H-metro) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: H-metro

item.title: Gvt Pledges Fresh Start for Returning Citizens @263Chat
item.description: By Kudzaishe Chimonera Government says it is putting measures in place to help returnees from South Africa find their feet again by identifying their skills, connecting them with jobs and offering support to those who need a fresh start. Speaking during a post cabinet briefing, [...] The post Gvt Pledges Fresh Start for Returning Citizens appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: ZIFA suspends five match officials
item.description: Tawah Munthali Zimpapers SportsHub THE Zimbabwe Football Association (ZIFA) has suspended five match officials after determining that their officiating decisions materially affected the outcomes of matches in the Premier Soccer... (via Ebusiness Weekly) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Ebusiness Weekly

item.title: Women Demand Action on Peace and Equality @263Chat
item.description: Women leaders have called for urgent action to translate the country’s gender equality commitments into tangible improvements in communities warning that policies alone will not deliver lasting peace or protect vulnerable groups. The calls were made during a recently held Women, Peace and Security Indaba [...] The post Women Demand Action on Peace and Equality appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: President Mnangagwa Appoints Geo Pomona Boss Dilesh Nguwaya as Special Advisor on Waste Management
item.description: Dilesh Nguwaya Takes on New Role as President Mnangagwa’s Special Advisor on Waste Management President Emmerson Mnangagwa has appointed Geo Pomona Waste Management executive chairman and chief executive Dr Dilesh Nguwaya as his Special Advisor on Waste Management and Environmental Sustainability, with immediate effect. The appointment is expected to strengthen Zimbabwe’s environmental management efforts as [...] The post President Mnangagwa Appoints Geo Pomona Boss Dilesh Nguwaya as Special Advisor on Waste Management appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Zimbabwe’s Women Farmers Struggle for Loans, Technology and Land Rights Despite Producing Much of the Nation’s Food @263Chat
item.description: For many women farmers, securing agricultural finance remains out of reach. The post Zimbabwe’s Women Farmers Struggle for Loans, Technology and Land Rights Despite Producing Much of the Nation’s Food appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: 5 Zimbabwe Football Match Officials Banned After ZIFA Investigation After Controversial Decisions
item.description: Zifa Suspends Five Match Officials Over Decisions That Affected Match Results The Zimbabwe Football Association (ZIFA) has suspended five match officials after determining that officiating decisions in several domestic league matches materially affected the outcomes of those fixtures. The suspensions, announced on Wednesday, 15 July 2026, follow reports submitted by match commissioners and affect officials [...] The post 5 Zimbabwe Football Match Officials Banned After ZIFA Investigation After Controversial Decisions appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Zimbabwe Repatriates Nearly 100 000 From SA As Women And Children Make Up Majority Of Returnees
item.description: More Than 70 Percent Of Zimbabweans Returning From South Africa Are Women And Children Zimbabwe has repatriated almost 100 000 citizens from South Africa as authorities respond to a growing number of people returning home following anti-migrant unrest. The Government says more than 70 percent of those assisted are women and children, with nearly 100 [...] The post Zimbabwe Repatriates Nearly 100 000 From SA As Women And Children Make Up Majority Of Returnees appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News
